questions.clue: In music, this term describes an ensemble of exactly seven performers — whether vocalists, instrumentalists, or a combination of both.

citations.title: Septet
citations.snippet: A septet is a formation containing exactly seven members. It is commonly associated with musical groups but can be applied to any situation where seven similar or related objects are considered a single unit, such as a seven-line stanza of

questions.clue: Its name is the Latin word for 'trumpet,' though this large brass instrument plays in the bass-to-contrabass range and descended from earlier instruments called the ophicleide and the serpent.

citations.title: Tuba
citations.snippet: The tuba is a large brass instrument in the bass-to-contrabass range. It is a member of the valved bugles, a large and diverse family of instruments characterized by their wide conical bore and use of valves to alter pitch. The tuba usually

questions.clue: Appointed Master of the King's Musick in 1924, this English composer wrote the Enigma Variations and a set of marches whose first trio became the melody most American graduation ceremonies use.

citations.title: Edward Elgar
citations.snippet: Sir Edward William Elgar, 1st Baronet was an English composer, many of whose works have entered the British and international classical concert repertoire. Among his best-known compositions are orchestral works including the Enigma Variatio

questions.clue: As lead singer of the Supremes, she fronted Motown's most successful act of the 1960s and the best-charting female group in recording history, scoring 12 number-one pop singles on the Billboard Hot 100.

citations.title: Diana Ross
citations.snippet: Diana Ross is an American singer and actress. She was the lead singer of the vocal group the Supremes, who became Motown's most successful act during the 1960s and one of the world's best-selling girl groups of all time. They remain the bes

questions.clue: Named for a Manhattan neighborhood known as a gayborhood, this American disco group was conceived by French producers Jacques Morali and Henri Belolo to celebrate gay culture through costumes representing masculine archetypes.

citations.title: Village People
citations.snippet: Village People is an American disco group known for its on-stage costumes and suggestive lyrics in their music. The group was originally formed by French producers Jacques Morali and Henri Belolo and lead singer Victor Willis following the

questions.clue: This 1977 film starring John Travolta as a Brooklyn disco dancer helped popularize disco music worldwide; its soundtrack featuring songs by the Bee Gees is one of the best-selling album soundtracks in history.

citations.title: Saturday Night Fever
citations.snippet: Saturday Night Fever is a 1977 American dance drama film directed by John Badham and produced by Robert Stigwood. It stars John Travolta as Tony Manero, a young Italian-American man who spends his weekends dancing and drinking at a local di

questions.clue: While serving as director of the National Conservatory of Music in New York City in the 1890s, this Czech composer wrote his most celebrated orchestral work, the Symphony 'From the New World.'

citations.title: Antonín Dvořák
citations.snippet: Antonín Leopold Dvořák was a Czech composer. He frequently employed rhythms and other aspects of the folk music of Moravia and his native Bohemia, following the Romantic era nationalist example of his predecessor Bedřich Smetana. Dvořák's s

questions.clue: This Italian term, meaning 'pinched,' describes the technique of playing bowed string instruments by plucking the strings with the fingers rather than drawing a bow across them.

citations.title: Pizzicato
citations.snippet: Pizzicato is a playing technique that involves plucking the strings of a string instrument. The exact technique varies somewhat depending on the type of instrument:On bowed string instruments it is a method of playing by plucking the string

questions.clue: Stephen Sondheim and James Lapine's 1987 Tony winner for Best Score weaves together the fates of characters from Cinderella, Rapunzel, and Jack and the Beanstalk to explore the darker consequences of each character's wish.

citations.title: Into the Woods
citations.snippet: Into the Woods is a 1986 musical with music and lyrics by Stephen Sondheim and book by James Lapine.

questions.clue: Jonathan Demme directed this 1984 concert film, shot over four nights at the Pantages Theatre in Hollywood as the Talking Heads promoted their fifth studio album, 'Speaking in Tongues.'

citations.title: Stop Making Sense
citations.snippet: Stop Making Sense is a 1984 American concert film featuring a live performance by the American rock band Talking Heads. The film was directed by Jonathan Demme and executive produced by Gary Kurfirst, the band’s longtime manager. The film w

questions.clue: Born Chaim Witz in Israel, this rock musician co-founded Kiss in 1973 alongside Paul Stanley and became famous for his 'Demon' stage persona and his remarkably long tongue.

citations.title: Gene Simmons
citations.snippet: Gene Simmons, also known by his stage persona "the Demon", is an Israeli and American musician. He was the bassist and co-lead singer of the hard rock band Kiss, which he co-founded with Paul Stanley, Ace Frehley, and Peter Criss in 1973. S

questions.clue: He won the Academy Award for Best Director for the musical drama Cabaret in 1972 and the Palme d'Or at Cannes for All That Jazz in 1979, making him arguably the most decorated figure in jazz dance of the 20th century.

citations.title: Bob Fosse
citations.snippet: Robert Louis Fosse was an American choreographer, dancer, actor, filmmaker, and stage director. He is known for his work on stage and screen, and was arguably the most influential figure in the field of jazz dance in the twentieth century.

questions.clue: This Hungarian Romantic composer coined the concept of the symphonic poem, invented the modern piano masterclass as a teaching format, and generated such hysterical devotion during his 1830s European tours that a new word was coined from his name.

citations.title: Franz Liszt

questions.clue: Frank Wedekind's 1891 German play about adolescent sexuality, long considered too scandalous for the stage, was adapted into a 2006 Broadway rock musical that won eight Tony Awards including Best Musical.

citations.title: Spring Awakening (musical)
citations.snippet: Spring Awakening is a coming-of-age rock musical with music by Duncan Sheik and a book and lyrics by Steven Sater. It is based on the 1891 German play Spring Awakening by Frank Wedekind. Set in late 19th-century Germany, the musical tells t

questions.clue: Revered as Finland's national composer, this man produced no major works in the last three decades of his life — a mysterious self-imposed halt his countrymen named after the lakeside town where he spent his final years.

citations.title: Jean Sibelius
